blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 8, Selection 40: write the selection correctly.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Begin Lesson 11. First look at the meanings of the roots for Lesson 11. Then look at the Lesson 11 Key Words. Which do you knowwithout looking at the definitions? Use the roots to help you. Read over the entries for each of the key words. Add the key words toyour notebook, so that you practice spelling them. Write down definitions for any words you do not already know.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 8
blocks.assignment_text: Read Adam of the Road.

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Work on the next 6 lines, up to "... with music." Review the previous lines.Review the poems you learned in Kindergarten (see the Poetry List for Kindergarten to Eighth Grade found at the end of theselesson plans). Recite as many as you can from memory. If you have not already memorized these poems or cannot recite them, thatis fine, just read and enjoy them.

blocks.subject: Art 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read Unit 4, Chapter 18, The Exuberant Style.

blocks.subject: Reading 8
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.assignment_text: Work for 5 minutes or less reviewing the four cardinal virtues. Discuss Chapter 21 of Our Life in the Church using "Words to Know"and the discussion questions in Chapter 21 Review in Our Life and the Church Quiz Book (see syllabus resources). The exercisescould be used for additional discussion or to encourage independent work. (Note: one could also use the questions in Chapter 21 ofthe activity book as a guide.) These can be done orally. The discussion will prepare you for the summaries in the next two days.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 8
blocks.assignment_text: Write the best part of the story you just finished reading. If possible write this in the first person as if you were one of the charactersin the story. Just tell that part of the story as if you were telling it to a friend, but do it in writing. If this is too difficult, then you can writethe best part of the story in the third person as a narration.Assessment Assignment:Week 21, Day 1, Adam of the Road Retelling #1

parsed.raw_text:
Weekly List for John Paul McAdams
Monday, April 27, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 87
Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church)Work for 5 minutes or less reviewing the seven capital sins. Read Chapter 21 of Our Life in the Church.
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Do workbook pages 168-169. (T.E. pages 340-343.)
Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2)Lesson 8, Selection 40: write the selection correctly.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Begin Lesson 11. First look at the meanings of the roots for Lesson 11. Then look at the Lesson 11 Key Words. Which do you knowwithout looking at the definitions? Use the roots to help you. Read over the entries for each of the key words. Add the key words toyour notebook, so that you practice spelling them. Write down definitions for any words you do not already know.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Read Adam of the Road.
Poetry 8 SyllabusWork on the next 6 lines, up to "... with music." Review the previous lines.Review the poems you learned in Kindergarten (see the Poetry List for Kindergarten to Eighth Grade found at the end of theselesson plans). Recite as many as you can from memory. If you have not already memorized these poems or cannot recite them, thatis fine, just read and enjoy them.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2)Read Unit 4, Chapter 18, The Exuberant Style.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour. 
 
Tuesday, April 28, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 88
Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church)Work for 5 minutes or less reviewing the four cardinal virtues. Discuss Chapter 21 of Our Life in the Church using "Words to Know"and the discussion questions in Chapter 21 Review in Our Life and the Church Quiz Book (see syllabus resources). The exercisescould be used for additional discussion or to encourage independent work. (Note: one could also use the questions in Chapter 21 ofthe activity book as a guide.) These can be done orally. The discussion will prepare you for the summaries in the next two days.
Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2)Lesson 8, Selection 41: find the errors and mark them in your book.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Look at the Familiar Words and Challenge Words for each root word in Lesson 11. If there are any you do not know, or can't figureout, look them up. You can add them to your notebook. Go over any of the Key Words you had trouble spelling.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Read Adam of the Road.
Poetry 8 SyllabusMemorize and review for five minutes.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour. 
 
 
Wednesday, April 29, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Test 21
Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church)Work for 5 minutes or less reviewing the corporal works of mercy. Then write a short summary of the various kinds of law, whichconstitute the external guide of the moral law.Assessment Assignment:Week 20, Day 3, Summary of the Kinds of Law
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Do Adjective Review on workbook pages 170-172. (T.E. pages 344-349.)
Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2)Lesson 8, Selection 41: write the selection correctly.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Do the Lesson 11 Exercises. Go over any Lesson 11 Key Words you had trouble spelling.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Geography: Complete Chapter 5 (South America), Section 1 (The Continent) in Eighth Grade Geography.OR If you have The Ultimate Geography and Timeline Guide: Read pp.169-170. Then, using a photocopy of the map on p. 233,identify the countries listed on p.169 and the items listed under ‘Map-It' on p.170.
Poetry 8 SyllabusMemorize and review for five minutes.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-1928 1-10)Listen to the Arrival of the Queen of Sheba by Handel.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour. 
 
Thursday, April 30, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 89
Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church)Work for 5 minutes or less reviewing the spiritual works of mercy. Then write a short summary of the correct understanding ofconscience.
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Today review anything in the adjective section that you had difficulty with. Also review any mistakes you made in the Adjective Review.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Have someone read you the definition of the Lesson 11 Key Words and you write down the word. If you had trouble spelling any ofthem, practice.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Finish Adam of the Road.
Poetry 8 SyllabusMemorize and review for five minutes, then recite as much of the section as you now know.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour. 
 
 
Friday, May 1, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 90
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Take Adjective Test on page 23 in the Test Booklet. (T.E. pages 350-353.)
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Take a spelling test on Lesson 11 Key words. If there are definitions you are unsure of, read them over again.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Write the best part of the story you just finished reading. If possible write this in the first person as if you were one of the charactersin the story. Just tell that part of the story as if you were telling it to a friend, but do it in writing. If this is too difficult, then you can writethe best part of the story in the third person as a narration.Assessment Assignment:Week 21, Day 1, Adam of the Road Retelling #1
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2)Optional: Do Art Pad #18, Night Sky Project, Part 2: Extreme View Perspective.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour.

Paul spoke to the multitude, and they listened until what point? Why did this make them so angry?2. The centurion was going to scourge and torture Paul. Why didn’t he do it?Practice defending your faith. Have someone ask you one or more of the questions you have been working on in your FriendlyDefender cards. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.History & Geog 7 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 3Write.Reading 7 Syllabus - Week: 19, Day: 2Read for about 1 hour.4/19/26, 8:36 PMPage 3 of 8

Take Quiz 21.History & Geog 7 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 4Write.Science 6 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 6) Syllabus - Week: 24, Day: 1Do questions 1-5 of the Review of Geology in your workbook.Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 14, Day: 1Work on the last 3½ lines. Review the first lines.Review the poems you learned in Sixth Grade (see the Poetry List for Kindergarten to Eighth Grade found at the end of these lessonplans). Recite as many as you can from memory. If you have not already memorized these poems or cannot recite them, that is fine,just read and enjoy them.Reading 7 Syllabus - Week: 19, Day: 3Read for about 1 hour.4/19/26, 8:36 PMPage 4 of 8

blocks.assignment_text: Listen to Charpentier's "Prélude pour le Te Deum" and "Regina Coeli"

blocks.subject: Reading 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Math 87
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 69

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Today go over workbook page 101 with your teacher. Then do workbook pages 102-104. (T.E. pages 192-199.)

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Have someone read you the definition of the Key Words for Lesson 10 and you write down the word. If you had trouble spelling anyof them, practice.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 21. Do the Day 3 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 7
blocks.assignment_text: Write.

blocks.subject: Reading 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Math 87
blocks.assignment_text: Review Lessons 47-69

blocks.subject: Religion 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read Chapter 23 of Acts. Also, start working on two new Friendly Defender cards. Pick one card from each of the followingcategories: Eucharist/Mass and Papacy. Read the cards and think about the issues. Start to memorize the verses.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Do the Lesson 9 & Lesson 10 Review Exercises.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 21. Take Quiz 21.

blocks.subject: History & Geog 7
blocks.assignment_text: Write.

blocks.subject: Science 6
blocks.assignment_text: Do questions 1-5 of the Review of Geology in your workbook.

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Work on the last 3½ lines. Review the first lines.Review the poems you learned in Sixth Grade (see the Poetry List for Kindergarten to Eighth Grade found at the end of these lessonplans). Recite as many as you can from memory. If you have not already memorized these poems or cannot recite them, that is fine,just read and enjoy them.

blocks.subject: Reading 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Math 87
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 70

blocks.subject: Religion 7
blocks.assignment_text: Discuss the questions for Chapter 23. Or, if you prefer, you may write out your answers.1. What device did Paul use to deflect interest from himself?2. How did Paul escape from the plot of the Jews?3. Where was Paul sent?4. Did the governor hear Paul's case immediately?Review your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards you worked on previously.

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Do the Verb Review today, pages 105-107. (T.E. pages 200-205.)

blocks.subject: Editing 7
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 7, Selection 35: find the errors and mark them in your book.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Take a spelling test on the Lesson 9 and Lesson 10 Key Words.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Attend Class. Do the Lesson XXI Quia Games.

blocks.subject: Science 6
blocks.assignment_text: Do questions 6-9 of the Review of Geology in your workbook. Review any concepts you had difficulty with.

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Memorize and review for five minutes.

blocks.subject: Art 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read Unit 4, Chapter 17, French Baroque Artists.

blocks.subject: Music 7
blocks.assignment_text: Listen to Handel's "Dixit Dominus"

blocks.subject: Reading 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

blocks.subject: Math 87
blocks.assignment_text: Investigation 7

blocks.subject: Religion 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read Chapter 24 of Acts. Review your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards youworked on previously.

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Do the Verb Review today, pages 108-110. (T.E. pages 206-211.)

blocks.subject: Editing 7
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 7, Selection 35: write the selection correctly.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Take Test: Lessons 9 and 10 from Vocabulary From Classical Roots B Test booklet.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 22. Review distinguishing between helping verbs and "to be" verbs (lesson XX). Read the twenty-second lesson. Oral Drill:decline the adjective "magnus, a, um" and the noun "dux, ducis." Then put them together, remembering that "dux" is a masculinethird declension word. Do the Day 1 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Oral Drill Answer Key:m: magnus, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magni, magnorum, magnis, magnos, magnisf: magna, magnae, magnae, magnam, magna, magnae, magnarum, magnis, magnas, magnisn: magnum, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magna, magnorum, magnis, magna, magnisdux, ducis, duci, ducem, duce, duces, ducum, ducibus, duces, ducibusmagnus dux, magni ducis, magno duci, magnum ducem, magno duce, magni duces, magnorumducum, magnis ducibus,magnos duces, magnis ducibus

blocks.subject: History & Geog 7
blocks.assignment_text: Finish writing.

blocks.subject: Science 6
blocks.assignment_text: Take the Test on Geology (this can be printed off from here or taken online).Assessment Assignment:Science 6 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science): Geology Test

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Memorize and review for five minutes.

blocks.subject: Art 7
blocks.assignment_text: Optional: Do Art Pad #17, Night Sky Project, Part 1: Tints and Shades.

blocks.subject: Reading 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.
